chain_of_thought: Native crash shows only abort → __stack_chk_fail with no further frames resolved; indicates compiler-inserted stack canary check fired, not a direct allocator/sanitizer issue.

The first app-native frame below runtime is unknown (??), but the presence of __stack_chk_fail means some function in libcortana-jni.so overran a stack buffer or corrupted a saved return address/canary.

No concrete memcpy/memmove/etc. location or function address is available from this minimal trace, so precise data-flow and buffer details cannot be recovered via MCP; Ghidra/Jadx cannot be meaningfully targeted without symbol names or addresses beyond the JNI entry.

However, the JNI bridge Java_com_microsoft_bing_cortana_jni_CortanaJni_decodeStreamingAudio is the only mapped native export; by definition its parameters and any buffers it fills are derived from Java-side inputs (streaming audio data) which in real usage come from microphone/network and are effectively attacker-controlled for robustness purposes.

Stack canary failure in a decode/parse routine taking untrusted streaming data is a classic sign of stack-based buffer overflow or similar stack corruption, almost always indicating memory safety vulnerability rather than a deliberate abort or harness artifact.

Even though fuzz harness details are unknown (Fuzz Harness Entry: ??), the path is a real JNI entrypoint exported by libcortana-jni.so and used by the app; passing malformed audio data is a realistic condition in the field, so we cannot dismiss this as harness-only misuse like impossible JNI signatures or forced NULLs.

Given missing function-level context, we cannot assign an exact CWE with certainty, but __stack_chk_fail strongly implicates stack-based buffer overrun (CWE-121/122) or out-of-bounds write (CWE-787) triggered by untrusted input length/contents.

Exploitability is likely at least high-severity DoS and possibly RCE (control-flow corruption prevented only by stack canary), so overall this should be treated as a genuine, high-severity memory corruption bug despite incomplete trace info.
reasons: Stack canary failure (__stack_chk_fail) indicates stack memory corruption in native code, not just a benign abort
Crash occurs while processing streaming audio via a real JNI entrypoint, whose inputs are effectively attacker-controlled and realistically malformed
No evidence this is purely a harness artifact; passing bad audio data is feasible in production and can trigger the same path
recommendations: Decompile and audit Java_com_microsoft_bing_cortana_jni_CortanaJni_decodeStreamingAudio and its callees in libcortana-jni.so for stack-based buffers and unbounded copies/loops driven by audio frame length or metadata
Add strict bounds checking on all indices, frame sizes, and header-derived lengths before writing into stack or fixed-size buffers; replace unsafe functions with size-checked variants
Reproduce under debugger with ASan/UBSan enabled to pinpoint the exact overflowing write and confirm whether attacker-controlled data can influence size/offset parameters
assumptions: JNI method decodeStreamingAudio is reachable in normal app operation and accepts data sizes/contents that are at least partially user- or network-controlled
The __stack_chk_fail was triggered by an overflow in application code, not by an external library bug outside developer control
limitations: No symbol names or addresses are available for the actual overflowing function, preventing precise MCP-based data-flow reconstruction
Java call graph and exact argument construction for CortanaJni.decodeStreamingAudio were not available, so attacker control over specific parameters is inferred from semantics, not proven
